# OlmPool LLM Context

OlmPool (https://www.olmpool.com) is an interactive research website for the OlmPool paper by Allen Institute for AI.

## What is OlmPool?

A controlled study of 26 comparable 7B LLMs trained with 170,000 H100 GPU hours, showing how minor architectural choices compound to reduce long-context performance by up to 47%.

Paper: "Cracks in the Foundation: Seemingly Minor Architectural Choices Impact Long Context Extension"
Authors: Bertsch, Soldaini, Gormley, Neubig, Hajishirzi, Lo, Groeneveld · Allen Institute for AI / CMU · April 2026

## Key Findings

- 47% performance drop from combining just 3 minor architectural choices
- Standard benchmarks are blind to long-context degradation
- NoPE + sliding window attention is the most dangerous combination
- Post-norm + QKV bias compounds the problem further

## Resources

- Paper: https://allenai.org/papers/olmpool
- Models: https://huggingface.co/collections/allenai/olmpool
